Title: Arabidopsis nitrate-induced aspartate oxidase gene expression is necessary to maintain metabolic balance under nitrogen nutrient fluctuation

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: SALK_013920 (mutant allele fin4-2 for AO knockout)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DNA microarray: GEO GSE136542; metabolomics by CE-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: de novo NAD+ biosynthesis entry/rate-controlling step
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; first committed de novo step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes L-aspartate to iminoaspartate in the first step of de novo NAD+ biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms an early committed intermediate and controls precursor entry toward NAD+ biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential redox cofactors for TCA cycle, mitochondrial electron transport, pentose phosphate pathway and photosynthesis-related redox metabolism; NAD+ also serves as substrate in ADP-ribosylation and deacetylation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: whole shoots (measured); broadly cellular pyridine nucleotide pools
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ao null mutant complementation with genomic AO driven by wild-type vs mutant AO promoter lacking the NLP-binding site; RT-qPCR; promoter transactivation in protoplasts; EMSA for NLP7 binding; metabolite profiling; pyridine nucleotide quantification; antimycin A sensitivity assay
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as key/rate-limiting step in de novo NAD+ biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Nitrate-induced AO expression is required to maintain metabolic balance under fluctuating nitrogen; loss of nitrate inducibility causes severe growth defects, altered TCA- and urea cycle-related metabolites, blunted transcriptome responses, and attenuated NAD(H)/NADP(H) adjustment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: N starvation; nitrate resupply (10 mM KNO3); continuous light; antimycin A; nicotinamide or nicotinate rescue
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AO promoter (wild type) and mutant AO promoter lacking the NLP-binding site; modified 35S promoter used in transient NLP7 effector assays
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transiently induced by nitrate in seedlings; promoter activity strong in leaves, root-shoot junctions, main root-lateral root junctions, root tips, and pollen grains
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Nitrate inducible in 4-day-old seedlings; induction abolished or strongly reduced when the promoter NLP-binding site is mutated
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of nitrate-inducible expression reduces shoot fresh weight and root length, causes narrower leaves, disconnected vascular loops, shorter root tips, shorter siliques, defective lateral root development, and increased sensitivity to antimycin A
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or engineering nitrate-responsive AO expression could help preserve metabolic balance and growth under fluctuating nitrogen; promoter integrity of the NLP-responsive cis-element is important
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite strong growth defects, AOpro(mut)-AO plants showed only limited decreases in NAD(H) and NADP(H) per unit fresh weight, suggesting compensation by reduced growth rate; nicotinamide/nicotinate partially rescue defects
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AO is a direct nitrate-responsive NLP target gene in Arabidopsis. 2. NLP7 binds an NRE-like cis-element (TGGCCGTT, -354 to -347) in the AO promoter to activate transcription in response to nitrate. 3. Selective loss of nitrate-induced AO expression causes growth and developmental defects with relatively small effects on pyridine nucleotide content per unit fresh weight. 4. The major metabolic consequence is disruption of TCA cycle- and urea cycle-related metabolite balance and attenuated transcriptional adaptation to nitrogen fluctuation.

Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Prior microarray evidence referenced; this study used DNA microarray (GEO GSE136542) mainly for AO downstream effects
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NODULE INCEPTION-LIKE PROTEIN transcription factor; RWP-RK DNA-binding domain; conserved N-terminal regulatory region
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Nitrate-dependent transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: de novo NAD+ biosynthesis gene expression in nitrate signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates AO transcription in the presence of nitrate by binding the AO promoter NRE-like site
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports redox metabolism and metabolic balance under nitrogen fluctuation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: nlp6 nlp7-1 mutant analysis; AO expression assays; protoplast cotransfection assays with NLP7 effector; EMSA with recombinant NLP7; mutant promoter analysis in planta
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes nitrate-induced AO expression and thereby supports growth and metabolic homeostasis during nitrogen fluctuation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Nitrate treatment after N starvation; continuous light
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct nitrate-activated regulator of AO, a key enzyme in de novo NAD+ biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated post-translationally by nitrate and accumulates in the nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduction of NLP function diminishes AO induction and is associated with higher antimycin A sensitivity in this context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulating NLP7-responsive promoter control could tune nitrate-coupled NAD biosynthesis and metabolic adaptation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NLP7 is the dominant NLP in Arabidopsis nitrate signaling, but other NLPs likely act redundantly
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NLP7 directly binds the AO promoter at TGGCCGTT (-354 to -347). 2. AO promoter activation by NLP7 requires nitrate. 3. NLP7-mediated regulation links nitrate signaling to de novo NAD+ biosynthesis and downstream metabolic balance.
